description: Mappings from Cyber Security Act 2024 (Act 854) provisions to Chief Executive Directives issued under s15

mappings.details: Enabling provision for all CE directives; s15(2) requires NCII entities and sector leads to comply

mappings.directive: Directive on NCII Entity Registration Procedures
mappings.details: Prescribes detailed procedures for NCII entity registration, designation acknowledgment, and transition requirements for existing operators under s57
Provides detailed risk assessment methodology requirements, threat categorization, vulnerability scoring, and risk calculation approaches

mappings.directive: Directive on Audit Standards and Frequency
mappings.details: Specifies audit standards, scope requirements, auditor competency criteria, and minimum audit frequency

mappings.directive: Directive on Incident Notification Timelines and Format
mappings.details: Provides detailed notification format templates, 'significant impact' classification criteria, escalation procedures, and follow-up reporting requirements

mappings.sectionTitle: Duty to provide information on threats
mappings.details: Establishes information sharing protocols, classification handling, secure channels, and trust frameworks for threat intelligence exchange

mappings.directive: Directive on Cyber Security Service Provider Obligations
mappings.details: Specifies professional conduct standards, reporting obligations, and operational requirements for licensed service providers
Provides guidance to sector leads on developing, structuring, and maintaining sector-specific codes of practice
Establishes coordinated vulnerability disclosure framework for NCII entities, including responsible disclosure timelines and procedures
Defines threat intelligence sharing formats, platforms, classification levels, and sharing protocols between NCII entities, sector leads, and NACSA
Provides framework for categorizing NCII assets by criticality, impact level, and dependency relationships
